meta.name: Thai-Style Coconut Rice
meta.subtitle: Fragrant jasmine rice cooked with coconut milk, lightly salted and subtly sweet—perfect with curries, grilled meats, and spicy stir-fries.

source.name: SSA Thai Catalog
notes: Designed as a versatile Thai-side starch for curry and stir-fry meals.
Balances coconut richness with salt so it pairs well with spicy mains.

summary: Coconut rice is jasmine rice cooked with coconut milk and water, seasoned lightly with salt (and optionally a touch of sugar). It’s a mellow, fragrant side that complements spicy Thai curries and stir-fries.

yield.notes: Makes about 6 cups cooked rice (varies slightly by rice brand and coconut milk).

notes: Refrigerates well 3–4 days; best reheated with a splash of water/steam.
Freezing is acceptable; texture will be slightly softer after thawing.

tasteProfile.notes: Keep sweetness low so it pairs well with savory/spicy dishes.

ingredients.label: Jasmine rice (uncooked)
ingredients.notes: About 2 cups. Rinse until water runs mostly clear for fluffier rice.

ingredients.label: Coconut milk (full-fat recommended)
ingredients.notes: 1 standard can. Shake well before measuring; avoid overly separated cans if possible.

ingredients.label: Water
ingredients.notes: Adjust slightly by rice brand; see notes in steps.

ingredients.label: Salt
ingredients.notes: About 1 tsp kosher salt (or ~1/2 tsp fine salt).

ingredients.label: Sugar (optional)
ingredients.notes: About 1 1/2 tsp. Use only if you want a slightly sweeter coconut profile.

ingredients.label: Pandan leaf (optional, tied in a knot)
ingredients.notes: Traditional fragrant add-in if available; remove after cooking.

ingredients.label: Lime zest (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds brightness. Use only as a finish right before serving.

steps.title: Rinse the rice
instructions: Place jasmine rice in a bowl and cover with cool water.
Swirl with your fingers, drain, and repeat 3–5 times until the water is mostly clear.
Drain well in a fine-mesh strainer for 1–2 minutes.
checks: Rice is rinsed to reduce excess surface starch.
Rice is well drained so liquid ratios stay consistent.

steps.title: Mix coconut cooking liquid
instructions: In the rice cooker pot (or stovetop pot), combine coconut milk and water.
Stir in salt and (optional) sugar until dissolved.
checks: Salt is dissolved; coconut milk is evenly mixed (no thick clumps).

steps.title: Cook the rice (rice cooker or stovetop)
instructions: Add rinsed rice to the coconut liquid and stir once to level.
Rice cooker method: cook on the standard 'White Rice' setting.
Stovetop method: bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat, stir once, cover tightly, reduce to low, and cook 15 minutes.
After cooking, keep covered and rest 10 minutes off heat (both methods).
checks: Rice grains are tender and liquid absorbed.
No scorching smell; if stovetop ran too hot, reduce next time.

steps.title: Fluff and finish
instructions: Fluff rice gently with a paddle or fork to release steam.
Optional: fold in lime zest right before serving for brightness.
checks: Rice is fluffy, not gummy; coconut aroma is present but balanced.

pairings.notes: Coconut rice is mellow—pair with spicy/sour mains for best contrast.

fridge.notes: Cool quickly, store airtight. Reheat with 1–2 tbsp water per cup and steam/microwave covered.

freezer.notes: Freeze in flat portions. Thaw overnight in fridge; reheat covered with a splash of water.

notes: Cool rice quickly to reduce food-safety risk; avoid holding warm rice at room temp.

adapt.notes: Use 500 ml coconut milk and reduce water to 140 ml (watch for brand differences; may cook softer).
Use 250 ml coconut milk and increase water to 390 ml. Coconut flavor is lighter, texture stays fluffy.

notes: Liquid ratios: this profile targets fluffy jasmine rice with coconut richness. If your rice brand runs firm, add 15–30 ml more water next time.
Avoid vigorous stirring after adding rice; it can make rice gummy.
If coconut milk is very thick/separated, whisk thoroughly before measuring.

aliases: ข้าวมันกะทิ

reviewNotes: All ingredients include id+label and amount.value/unit (or clearly optional).
All steps include id+title and instructions[]; timers use minutes.
